commit.message:
MENU-ENABLED-SUBSTRATE: registry disabled-but-visible enablement (editor-ui, additive)

First of two carves for the owner-approved "dynamic menu enablement" design. The
menu registry's `predicate` is VISIBILITY-only (a false predicate FILTERS the
entry out of the menu AND the accelerator index) — it has no "greyed-but-present"
concept; today that's a separate bespoke `MenuStateHandoff`/`play_item_enabled`
path used only for Play items, while PIE-gated File items look clickable but
silently no-op during Play. This dispatch adds the canonical enablement concept in
editor-ui as ADDITIVE, behaviour-neutral substrate; the follow-up
(MENU-DYNAMIC-RESOLVE) wires the host/shell to it + retires the bespoke channel.

- predicate.rs: extend `PredicateContext` (#[non_exhaustive], additive) with
  `can_play`/`can_pause`/`can_stop`/`can_step` (Play-item enablement, filled
  shell-side from `PlayState::can_*`) + `is_editing` (File Save/Open/Save-As gate).
- entry.rs: add a separate `enabled: Predicate` field to `MenuEntry` (default
  always-enabled) + `with_enabled` builder; clarify `predicate` is VISIBILITY
  (removes the entry) vs `enabled` is ENABLEMENT (greys but keeps it present +
  accelerator-bound).
- registry.rs: `ResolvedEntry.enabled: bool` computed in `resolve_slot` (NOT the
  Step-1 visibility filter — disabled entries stay in `by_point`); the
  `shortcut_commands` index now stores `(Command, enabled)`; `command_for_shortcut`
  keeps its binding semantics (W08 parity guard intact); NEW
  `enabled_command_for_shortcut` returns the command only when enabled (the
  resolver the keyboard execution path will use so disabled accelerators don't
  fire).
- default_menu.rs: attach the enablement predicates — File→`is_editing`, Play→the
  matching `can_*`; Edit/View stay always-enabled.
- Tests: a disabled-but-visible entry stays present with `enabled==false` while
  `command_for_shortcut` still returns its binding and `enabled_command_for_shortcut`
  returns None; default_menu enablement tracks editing/playing contexts; the new
  context booleans default false.

No consumer reads `enabled` yet (the host still projects the 3-tuple; the shell
keyboard still uses `command_for_shortcut`), so this is behaviour-neutral and the
consumers compile unchanged. editor-ui only; no Cargo/edge change.
Gate: VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints; editor-ui tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
